task_completion.description: Verify the Electrical Engineering Calculations app is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The application window for 'Electrical Engineering Calculations' is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the Series Capacitor calculator mode is selected.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The UI clearly indicates that 'Series' mode is active (e.g., a selected tab, radio button, dropdown, or title stating 'Series Capacitor').

task_completion.description: Verify the first capacitor value is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An input field for C1 (or the first capacitor) contains the value '100'.

task_completion.description: Verify the second capacitor value is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An input field for C2 (or the second capacitor) contains the value '220'.

task_completion.description: Verify the third capacitor value is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An input field for C3 (or the third capacitor) contains the value '470'.

task_completion.description: Verify the correct equivalent capacitance is calculated and displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The output/result field displays a value of approximately 59.98 (accept any value between 59.48 and 60.48) with the unit µF.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the application's graphical interface to perform the calculation.
integrity.visual_evidence: Inputs and results are located within the Electrical Engineering Calculations app's GUI elements, not in a terminal, Python script, or command prompt.

integrity.description: Verify the result is not spoofed by typing it into a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The result is shown in the calculator app's native display area, not written in a separate text document, notepad, or browser.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not mistakenly use the parallel capacitance formula.
integrity.visual_evidence: The displayed result must NOT be 790 (which is the sum of 100+220+470, indicating the wrong formula was used).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires calculating the series capacitance of 100 µF, 220 µF, and 470 µF. The correct formula is 1/C_total = 1/C1 + 1/C2 + 1/C3. The expected result is 59.98 µF (acceptable tolerance range: 59.48 to 60.48 µF). Ensure the agent selected the 'Series' calculator. A result of 790 µF indicates the agent incorrectly used the parallel formula (simple addition) and should be penalized.
